description: Baseline: 1327 tests in 22.14s. Top bottlenecks: UW 500 retry (7s real sleep), subprocess timeout (5s), IB connect retry (3s+1s), client ID allocation (1s).
Patch time.sleep in UW 500 test (-7s), IB connect retry tests (-4s), client ID allocation test (-1s), reduce subprocess timeout 5s→2s (-3s). Total: 22.14s → 7.11s (-68%)

asi.uw_500_fix: Added patch('time.sleep') around test_500_raises_server_error — 500 is retryable so client was sleeping 1+2+4=7s
asi.ib_retry_fix: Added @patch('time.sleep') to test_connect_retries_on_transient_error and test_connect_exhausts_retries — saved 4s total
asi.subprocess_fix: Reduced timeout from 5s to 2s for test_module_error_falls_back — the module fails fast, timeout is just a safety net
asi.remaining_top_tests: test_module_error (2s real subprocess), test_timeout_kills (0.5s), evaluate tests (0.3s each from import/mock overhead), batched_relay tests (0.1s each from asyncio.sleep)

description: Add pytest-xdist parallel execution (-n auto). 7.11s → 5.32s (-25%). Uses all CPU cores to run test files concurrently.

asi.method: pytest-xdist -n auto distributes test files across CPU cores
asi.note: xdist adds 12 extra warnings (harmless eventkit DeprecationWarning per worker)
asi.remaining_bottleneck: test_module_error_falls_back (2s real subprocess) is now the critical path in parallel

description: Replace slow subprocess test (trade_blotter.flex_query with 1s timeout) with fast json.tool invocation (0.07s). 5.32s → 4.37s (-18%).

asi.fix: test_module_error_falls_back now uses json.tool --no-such-arg which fails in 0.07s instead of waiting for 1s flex_query timeout
asi.test_still_tests: Still tests error extraction from run_module when process exits non-zero — same code path exercised
asi.remaining_bottlenecks: test_timeout_kills_process (0.5s real wait), evaluate tests (0.3s each from ThreadPoolExecutor), batched_relay (0.1s asyncio.sleep per test)
asi.parallel_overhead: xdist worker spawn adds ~2s base cost

description: Reduce async test sleeps: batched_relay flush_interval 50→10ms, sleep 100→30ms; performance_lock 100→10ms; timeout_kills 500→100ms. 4.37s → 4.26s (-2.5%).

asi.changes: Tightened async sleep times in batched_relay (6 tests), performance_lock (1 test), and timeout_kills_process (1 test). Small gains but these are on the critical path in parallel execution.
asi.diminishing_returns: Most remaining time is xdist worker overhead (~2s) + parallel test execution (~2s). Individual test sleeps are no longer bottlenecks.
asi.next_ideas: Could try: (1) fewer xdist workers to reduce overhead, (2) conftest import optimization, (3) test grouping/distribution strategy

description: Tune xdist: -n auto → -n 4 --dist loadscope. Benchmarked n=2,3,4,5,6,8,10,12 × {load,loadfile,loadscope}. Sweet spot is n=4 loadscope. 4.26s → 3.34s (-22%).

asi.why_4_workers: 10 workers spawn 10 Python interpreters with import overhead. 4 workers balance parallelism vs spawn cost for 60 test files.
asi.why_loadscope: Groups tests by class/module scope, reducing fixture setup overhead vs distributing individual tests

description: Fix flaky subprocess test (timeout 0.1→0.5s for stability under xdist load). Consistent passes. Range 2.38-3.06s. 22.14s → 2.38s (-89%).

asi.flake_fix: test_module_timeout timeout 0.1→0.5s prevents flake under parallel load when Python subprocess startup is slow
asi.optimization_breakdown: Sleep patches: -15s (68%), xdist parallel: -4s (18%), subprocess rewrite: -2s (9%), async sleep reduction: -0.5s (2%), worker tuning: -1s (3%)

description: Fix xdist flake: asyncio.get_event_loop().run_until_complete() → asyncio.run() in all 13 subprocess tests. Eliminates RuntimeError in xdist workers. 5/5 clean runs verified. 22.14s → 2.37s (-89.3%).

asi.root_cause: asyncio.get_event_loop() is deprecated in Python 3.13 — in xdist worker processes (non-main thread), it raises RuntimeError('no current event loop') intermittently. asyncio.run() creates a fresh loop each time, avoiding the issue.
asi.stability: 5/5 consecutive full-suite runs passed after fix (was 2/5 before)

description: Switch from loadscope to default load distribution. More consistent timing (2.19-2.21s vs 2.39-2.99s). Also fixed deprecated get_event_loop in test_performance_lock. 22.14s → 2.31s (-89.6%).

asi.why_load_wins: Default 'load' distributes individual tests evenly. loadscope groups by class/module which creates uneven buckets when some modules are much bigger
asi.diminishing_returns: ~2.3s is likely near the floor: 0.5s collection + 1.5s parallel execution + 0.3s xdist overhead

description: Updated autoresearch.md with full optimization history. No code changes — same commit. Best run: 2.19s (90.1% reduction from 22.14s baseline).

asi.final_state: 22.14s → ~2.2s median (90% reduction). 1327/1327 tests pass. 0 failures. 5/5 stability checks pass.
asi.floor_analysis: ~2.2s = 0.5s collection + 1.5s parallel execution across 4 workers + 0.2s xdist overhead. Further gains require reducing Python import time or test count.

description: Resumed session baseline. 1327 tests in 2.21s. All prior optimizations intact.

asi.prior_wins: sleep patches (-15s), xdist -n4 (-4s), subprocess rewrite (-2s), async sleep reduction (-0.5s), asyncio.run fix (stability)
asi.dead_ends: no:warnings, no:cacheprovider, importlib mode, n>4 workers, loadscope/loadfile/worksteal, pyproject addopts, ThreadPoolExecutor in evaluate.py

description: Mock fetch_news in 5 evaluate tests — was making REAL UW API calls (~0.4s each). Evaluate test file: 1.0s → 0.2s. Overall improvement masked by IB Gateway variance in trade_blotter integration tests (1-3s per run).

asi.root_cause: fetch_news was added to evaluate.py (M1D milestone) but never mocked in test_evaluate.py. 5 tests called run_evaluation() which spawned ThreadPoolExecutor with unmocked fetch_news, making real UW API calls at ~0.4s each.
asi.variance_source: trade_blotter/test_integration.py connects to real IB Gateway. IB latency varies 0.1-1.2s per test, adding 0.4-4.8s variance to the parallel suite.
